task.title: Track review yield, false-positive rate, cost/latency, time-to-green, and human review burden.

context.problem_statement: The harness already carries generic harness_metrics and finding dispositions, but it does not define review-yield, false-positive-rate, cost/latency, time-to-green, or human-review-burden fields. Without explicit outcome and effort metrics, the workflow can claim productivity from more findings or faster generation while hiding reviewer burden, noisy output, latency, or unmeasured costs.
background: AGW-037 added finding status/disposition values such as accepted, rejected, fixed, duplicate, and false-positive-related notes.
AGW-046 and AGW-047 added deterministic docs-impact and prompt-regression fixtures for false-positive and output-quality behavior.
AGW-071 documented the workflow stack as an agent harness and named human burden as a metric family.
AGW-074 added optional harness_metrics to task packets and session receipts, currently with simple counts and token_budget.
schemas/session-receipt.schema.json currently permits additional harness_metrics but only documents context_file_count, commands_run_count, changed_file_count, and token_budget.
scripts/verify_agent_receipt.py currently validates the existing integer metric fields when present.
scripts/render_session_receipt_summary.py summarizes findings, commands, tests, and disposition but does not render review outcome or effort metrics.
make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_INCLUDE_CLOSED=1 TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 at packet creation reported active_task_count 0, no hazards, no stale tasks, and no unknown-scope tasks.
make agent-token-budget at packet creation reported 104 files and 85776 estimated tokens, result passed.

make kit-status at packet creation reported installed repo-contract-kit 0.4.41, target repo version 0.2.22, runtime adapters none, and managed-file drift relative to installed kit metadata; this is source self-dogfood drift, not a blocker for source schema/docs work.
non_goals: Do not add a metrics database, dashboard, hosted telemetry, CI upload, or background collector.
Do not calculate organization-wide false-positive rates automatically from git history or external systems.
Do not make review outcome metrics mandatory for all receipts in this packet; keep existing receipts valid and allow unknown/not-collected states.
Do not add repo-contract-kit installed command behavior or target-repo templates.
Do not change reviewer persona finding semantics beyond pointing them to receipt metric capture.
Do not claim productivity improvements from these metrics; document interpretation caveats.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the source of prompt, schema, fixture, packaging, and harness-quality guidance for local agentic review and implementation.

area_contracts.purpose: Machine-readable source-side receipt contract for local agent run evidence.
Local strict receipt validator that should reject malformed metrics without making optional metrics mandatory.
Human-readable receipt summary surface where outcome and burden metrics should be visible when present.
Source-side harness map and metric-family guidance.
Prompt that tells synthesis reviewers how to capture receipt fields after persona review.

stop_conditions: Stop if implementation needs a metrics service, external API, hosted telemetry, or repo-contract-kit command behavior.
Stop if metrics become mandatory for receipts that cannot know them yet.
Stop if the schema permits negative counts, invalid rates, or ambiguous units for duration/cost fields.
Stop if docs present metrics as productivity proof rather than evidence requiring interpretation.
Stop if summary rendering leaks private model/provider/cost details that were not explicitly captured in the receipt.

protected_files: /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/schemas/task-packet.schema.json unless session metrics need a matching documentation-only reference
/Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/workflows/manifest.json unless prompt adapter export proves a manifest typo
/Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/.agent-workflows/ runtime state except reading deterministic reports
metrics storage, dashboards, hosted collectors, CI upload config, or account/tool credentials
unrelated backlog rows and unrelated dirty work
expected_outputs: session-receipt harness_metrics documents optional review outcome metrics: findings total, open/accepted/rejected/fixed/deferred/duplicate counts, false-positive count/rate, duplicate count/rate, review-yield count/rate, human decision count, and human approval/review burden fields.

session-receipt harness_metrics documents optional effort metrics with explicit units: duration or latency milliseconds, time-to-green milliseconds, model/provider cost fields when known, token usage or budget fields when known, and reviewer time or interruption counts when known.

Receipt validation rejects malformed optional metrics such as negative counts, rates outside 0..1, non-integer millisecond durations, or non-object metric groups, while preserving backward compatibility for receipts that omit metrics.
Receipt summary renders a compact Metrics section when review outcome or effort metrics are present and stays quiet when absent.
Review synthesis prompt tells agents to populate metrics from actual findings, dispositions, commands, timing, and known cost data; unknown metrics must be omitted or marked unknown, not guessed.
Docs explain that these metrics are evidence for calibration, not productivity proof, and require interpretation beside review quality and human burden.
Generated Codex prompt adapter mirrors any changed canonical prompt sources.
Version/changelog and source backlog closeout are updated after validation.

acceptance_criteria.description: schemas/session-receipt.schema.json defines optional review outcome and effort metric groups under harness_metrics with units and bounded rate/count semantics.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect schema and focused receipt validation tests.

acceptance_criteria.description: scripts/verify_agent_receipt.py validates optional metric groups when present and keeps receipts without these metrics valid.
scripts/render_session_receipt_summary.py renders a concise Metrics section for review outcome, false-positive, latency, time-to-green, cost, and human-burden fields when present.
Review synthesis and docs tell agents to capture metrics from actual evidence and avoid guessing unknown cost, latency, false-positive, or human-burden values.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect workflows/prompts/review-synthesis.md, docs/harness-engineering.md, README.md, and docs/using-the-prompt-kit.md if changed.

acceptance_criteria.description: Generated Codex adapters are refreshed if prompt sources changed.
The change does not add telemetry collection, dashboards, hosted upload, repo-contract-kit behavior, or mandatory metrics for all receipts.
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff --name-only compared with allowed/protected scope.

acceptance_criteria.description: Version/changelog and source backlog rows close AGW-048 only after validation passes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: make version-check, make backlog-check, and make backlog-split-check.

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the source checkout is clean, only expected files remain dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This changes receipt schema semantics and user-facing review workflow guidance.

known_risks: Metrics can incentivize more findings rather than better findings if docs do not include interpretation caveats.
Cost/latency and human-time fields can be unavailable or private; agents must not invent them.
Breaking receipt validation would invalidate existing local receipts.
Rates require bounded semantics and clear denominators to avoid misleading summaries.
stop_conditions: Implementation would make new metrics required for existing receipt modes.
Implementation would require a hosted metrics service, dashboard, or repo-contract-kit installed command.
Implementation cannot define bounded count/rate/duration semantics without ambiguity.
Validation finds generated adapter drift or unrelated dirty work outside this packet's scope.

approval.notes: User requested implementation of the planned workflow-stack backlog items; AGW-048 is the next selected open item after AGW-045 closeout.

overlap_warnings: Refresh git status and task-status before worker edits.
Do not edit repo-contract-kit or installed kit runtime state.
Treat installed kit managed-file drift as known self-dogfood state unless it becomes actual git dirt or affects validation.
